steps.title: Recall what the y-intercept is
steps.content:
The $y$-intercept is the point where the graph crosses the $y$-axis, i.e., where $x = 0$. Substitute $x = 0$ into the equation:
$$y = \frac{32}{0 - 4} = \frac{32}{-4} = -8$$
So the $y$-intercept is $(0, -8)$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $(0, -8)$: Setting $x = 0$ gives $y = 32/(-4) = -8$. ✓
  • Choice B — $(0, 0)$: Would require $32/(0-4) = 0$, but $32/(-4) = -8 \ne 0$. ✗
  • Choice C — $(-4, 0)$: This confuses the $x$-intercept with the $y$-intercept, and even as an $x$-intercept it is wrong — setting $y = 0$ gives $32/(x-4) = 0$, which has no solution (a fraction equals zero only if its numerator is zero, but 32 is never zero). ✗
  • Choice D — $(32, 0)$: Plugging in the numerator as an intercept is not a valid method. The $y$-intercept requires $x = 0$, not $x = 32$. ✗
That is the correct answer.
